weather.text: A soft rain begins to patter on the bunker roof.
The wind picks up — a storm is rolling in from the east.
Heavy clouds settle overhead. The daylight dims.
A hurricane warning crackles across the emergency band.
The sky clears. Sunlight pours onto the solar receivers.
A light fog rolls in, muffling the world above.
Hail taps against the steel plates overhead.
The air turns crisp and still. A quiet afternoon.

radio: 📻 ...to any station listening, this is Outpost-Seven. We are holding. Repeat: holding...
📻 ...scattered broadcasts from the coast — a convoy of survivors moving north along Route 9...
📻 ...static...  'please... if anyone hears this... we are at the old grain silo'  ...static...
📻 ...anonymous voice: 'stay underground tonight. do not look up at the sky.'
📻 ...a children's choir, impossibly clean. Then cut to static mid-verse.
📻 ...numbers station cycles through: seven — four — seven — fourteen — ninety-one — seven...
📻 ...old jazz record loops endlessly on an abandoned frequency.
📻 ...'this is Dr. Hale. the vaccine trial data is at coordinates'  ...signal lost...

front.text: 📡 Front report: zombie swarms regrouping near the river crossings. Quiet tonight.
📡 Front report: shambler activity down 40% since the cold snap began.
📡 Unverified sighting: lights in the sky, moving against the wind, two valleys over.
📡 A scout reports symbols burned into crop fields — perfect circles, clean edges.
📡 Militia channel warns of mindless humans roaming — no hostility, no speech, just walking.
📡 A man was found at a checkpoint this morning, humming. He could not stop humming.
📡 Salvage team encountered ambulatory machines — origin unknown. They did not respond.
📡 Industrial drones spotted drifting past the treeline, blue status lights flashing.
📡 Medical net flags a new cough in the northern settlements. Origin unclear.
📡 Three outposts quarantined overnight. No transmissions since the seal.

ambient: A slow drip finds its way into the floor drain. It's soothing, somehow.
The ventilation hums a single, steady note.
Dust drifts through the reading light above the workbench.
You log the day's entry in the margin of an old notebook.
Somewhere in the shelves, a paperback slips sideways and settles.
The bunker creaks as the earth above shifts.
